text: author1, author2, author3 (publicationYear) articleTitle, *journalName*; volumeNumber, [linkToPublication](linkToPublication)
code: @Article{,   entry = {Article},   title = {articleTitle},   author = {{author1} and {author2} and {author3}},   journal = {journalName},   year = {publicationYear},   volume = {volume},   number = {number},   pages = {pages},   url = {linkToPublication}, }

what: A package for identification through heteroskedasticity a la Lewbel (2012)

title: A package for identification through heteroskedasticity a la Lewbel (2012)
text: project explores various approaches identifying testing heteroskedasticity econometric models, particular attention modern identification strategies.

title: A package for identification through heteroskedasticity a la Lewbel (2012)
text: Lewbel, . (2012). Using heteroscedasticity identify estimate mismeasured endogenous regressor models. Journal Business & Economic Statistics, 30(1), 67-80.

title: NA
text: Open R session (e.g., RStudio via Rscript) run:
code: # Install from CRAN install.packages("rworkflows")  # (Optional) Install the latest development version: # remotes::install_github("neurogenomics/rworkflows")

title: NA
text: templateR GitHub repo provides CRAN-compatible scaffold. Merge necessary files:
code: # 1. Add templateR as a temporary remote git remote add templateR https://github.com/neurogenomics/templateR.git git fetch templateR  # 2. Check out scaffold files into your branch git checkout templateR/main -- \   DESCRIPTION \   NAMESPACE \   inst/ \   man/ \   R/000-package.R \   .github/workflows/templateR.yaml  # 3. Remove the temporary remote git remote remove templateR

title: NA
text: Open DESCRIPTION editor merge update fields: Also ensure LICENSE file (e.g., MIT) CRAN-related metadata (e.g., cran-comments.md desired).
code: Package:        YOURPACKAGENAME Title:          A Package for X in Econometrics Version:        0.1.0 Authors@R:      person("First", "Last",                       email = "you@example.com",                       role = c("aut", "cre")) Description:    Brief description of what your package does. License:        MIT + file LICENSE Encoding:       UTF-8 LazyData:       true Roxygen:        list(markdown = TRUE)

title: NA
text: Back R, run: creates updates .github/workflows/rworkflows.yaml : Run R CMD check across platforms Build vignettes pkgdown site (Optionally) Build push Docker container Perform Bioconductor checks, Python support, etc.

title: NA
text: Open .github/workflows/rworkflows.yaml. Confirm triggers (push, pull_request) cover development branches. econometrics code needs system libraries, add setup jobs: sections.

title: NA
text: R, run: ensures : Roxygen2 documentation (R/ auto-generated man/) tests/testthat/ placeholder test file vignettes/getting-started.Rmd basic pkgdown config _pkgdown.yml
code: usethis::use_roxygen_md()        # Markdown-friendly Roxygen2 usethis::use_testthat()          # testthat unit testing usethis::use_vignette("getting-started")  # starter vignette usethis::use_pkgdown()           # pkgdown website

what: 8. Commit, Push & Open a Pull Request
title: NA
text: open PR GitHub review.
code: git add . git commit -m "Add templateR scaffold and rworkflows CI" git push -u origin add-rworkflows

title: NA
text: PR checks pass (build, tests, R CMD check, pkgdown), merge main. GitHub Actions, confirm see rworkflows job run successfully pkgdown site (configured) builds/deploys. 🎉 repository now : CRAN-compatible package scaffold templateR modern rworkflows GitHub Action pipeline (checks, docs, Docker, Bioconductor) Roxygen2, testthat, vignettes, pkgdown configured ’re ready continue developing econometrics data-analysis package automated, reproducible CI/CD place!
